channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: China's population falls for a third straight year, posing challenges for its government and economy
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China's population fell last year for the third straight year, its government said Friday, pointing to further demographic challenges for the world's second most populous nation, which is now facing both an aging population and an emerging shortage of working age people.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ohio State wins 1st national title since 2014, outlasting Notre Dame 34-23 in CFP championship game
item.description: Instead of crying over another collapse, Ohio State can celebrate another national title after holding off a Notre Dame comeback bid Monday night to walk away with a nailbiter of a 34-23 victory over the Fighting Irish.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thailand gears up for a celebration as a long-awaited marriage equality law takes effect
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — They have been in a committed relationship for more than 13 years, and even had a wedding in 2019. Since then, Danaya Phonphayung and Sunma Piamboon, both women, have considered themselves a married couple, even if same-sex marriages were not legally recognized.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump sets out to erase Biden's legacy with pardons and orders immediately after taking office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump began erasing Joe Biden ’s legacy immediately after taking office as the nation’s 47th president on Monday, pardoning nearly all of his supporters who rioted at the U.S. Capitol on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Authorities say a US border patrol agent was fatally shot in Vermont south of border
item.description: COVENTRY, Vt. (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent was fatally shot Monday on a highway in northern Vermont south of the Canadian border, authorities said. The death was confirmed by the FBI and Benjamine Huffman, acting secretary of the Department of Homeland Security in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Fire crews in Southern California quickly extinguish brush fires amid extreme fire weather
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters quickly extinguished several brush fires that erupted Monday in Southern California amid windy and dry conditions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrat congressman condemns 'what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute' from Musk
item.description: Rep. Jerry Nadler (D-N.Y.) was among those who sharply criticized billionaire Elon Musk on Monday for making "what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute" during an event celebrating the inauguration of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate passes Laken Riley Act in first move after Trump inauguration
item.description: The Senate on Monday passed the Laken Riley Act, making the immigration-related bill the first piece of legislation to make it through the upper chamber in the new Congress and putting it a step closer to being signed into law by President Trump. Senators voted 64-35 on the bill.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: Slideshow: President's supporters show out in foam cowboy hats and Trump-adorned leggings
item.description: (KRON) – The hat worn by First Lady Melania Trump may have captured the world’s attention at President Donald J. Trump’s inauguration on Monday, but backers of the newly sworn-in 47th president also made bold fashion statements in his honor—both at his pre-inauguration rally and on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KRON4

item.title: Man parachutes off TV tower in Weld County
item.description: "In case you didn't know, jumping from a TV tower is not only incredibly dangerous, but it is trespassing," the sheriff's office stated.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk already has a White House email address
item.description: "It seems to confirm he's going to have an EEOB office," the source said, using the acronym for Eisenhower Executive Office Building.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, NewsNation

item.title: Former Planned Parenthood president, women's rights activist Cecile Richards has died at 67
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — Cecile Richards, a national leader for abortion access and women’s rights who led Planned Parenthood for 12 tumultuous years, has died. She was 67. Richards died Monday at home in New York “surrounded by family and her ever-loyal dog, Ollie,” her family said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Can Trump really end birthright citizenship? Expert breaks down inauguration speech promises
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- A lot was said during President Donald J. Trump’s inauguration speech—ranging from tariffs, drilling, and deportations to executive orders, “the Gulf of America,” and gender identity rights.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KRON4

item.title: MLK Day at King's Atlanta congregation features daughter's warning about anti-woke rhetoric
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A massive 70-member choir belted out “Hallelujah” at a Martin Luther King Jr. Day service Monday at his former congregation in Atlanta, followed by a stern message from his youngest daughter warning against anti-woke rhetoric.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's who skipped Trump's inauguration
item.description: President Trump was sworn into office for a second time on Monday before an audience in the Capitol that included his predecessor, former President Biden, as well as former Presidents Obama, Clinton and George W. Bush and multiple current and former officials and lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: Carrie Underwood sings 'America the Beautiful' a cappella after apparent tech issue at inauguration
item.description: An apparent technical issue didn't stop Carrie Underwood from delivering "America the Beautiful" during the second inauguration of President Trump on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Businessman found liable for $120 million in damages at special needs trust company
item.description: A federal bankruptcy judge ordered Clearwater businessman, Leo Govoni, to be responsible for the missing $120 million.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Muller, NewsNation

item.title: Costco workers authorize nationwide strike
item.description: Thousands of Costco workers nationwide voted Sunday to authorize a strike if a contract is not reached by the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Melania dons hat at Trump inauguration, struggles to kiss Trump
item.description: First lady Melania Trump wore a navy and ivory ensemble by American designer Adam Lippes for the inauguration ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to issue 10 executive orders cracking down on border
item.description: President-elect Trump is set to sign off on 10 executive orders Monday, moving swiftly to crack down on immigration and drug cartels during his first day in office with a blitzkrieg of immigration actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Couple with fake fire truck busted for impersonating firefighters near Palisades Fire in Los Angeles: sheriff
item.description: A couple from Oregon was busted for impersonating firefighters near the Palisades Fire in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Massachusetts officials suspect bird flu killed dozens of birds in Plymouth
item.description: Massachusetts officials suspect more than 60 birds found dead in Plymouth died from bird flu, though official results may not come for days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Can you spot black ice? What to do if you start sliding or skidding
item.description: Black ice forms without creating bubbles, meaning it blends in with the road and becomes hard to detect.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, Fox 5

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Over 100 out-of-state homeless in Hawaii sent back to mainland families
item.description: Hawaii lawmakers claim programs from other states send homeless to the Islands, especially in the winter.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Moore, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden pardons the late Black nationalist Marcus Garvey as well as 4 others and commutes 2 sentences
item.description: President Joe Biden on Sunday posthumously pardoned Black nationalist Marcus Garvey, who influenced Malcolm X and other civil rights leaders and was convicted of mail fraud in the 1920s.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Coco Gauff writes 'RIP TikTok USA' on a TV camera at the Australian Open
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — American tennis star Coco Gauff mourned the loss of TikTok's app back home, writing on a TV camera lens “RIP TikTok USA” and drawing a broken heart right after winning a match at the Australian Open to reach the quarterfinals.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Stephen Curry considered day to day after injuring left ankle in win over Wizards
item.description: Stephen Curry injured his left ankle in the final minutes of the Golden State Warriors victory over Washington on Saturday night and is considered day to day.
item.dc:creator: ERIC HE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texans' Kris Boyd flings helmet, gets penalized and then shoves his coach on first play vs. Chiefs
item.description: Texans cornerback Kris Boyd nearly shoved Houston special teams coach Frank Ross to the ground after the opening kickoff of their divisional playoff loss to the Kansas City Chiefs on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California officials warn against price gouging as rents soar in fire-stricken Los Angeles
item.description: Southern California's expensive housing market is going to get a lot more competitive after deadly firestorms torched more than 12,000 homes and other structures in the Los Angeles area, leaving tens of thousands of people without a place to stay.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Most voters in new survey seek toned-down Trump agenda
item.description: Over 60% of Americans polled disagree with Trump's idea to replace thousands of career civil-service workers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Chicago prepares for deportation arrests targeting hundreds next week after Trump takes office
item.description: Federal immigration officers will target more than 300 people with histories of egregious, violent crimes after President-elect Donald Trump takes office Monday, an official said, marking his administration's initial attempt toward fulfilling his promise of large-scale deportations.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Stray dog brings sick, freezing puppy to vet's office
item.description: A stray dog was caught on camera carrying her sick puppy to the front door of a veterinarian clinic in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey teacher charged with allegedly sexually assaulting former student, having his child
item.description: A New Jersey teacher is facing charges after police say she is accused of sexually abusing one of her students and having his child.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Cristelli, Fox 5
